name: Old Greek Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 packages dry yeast, prefer quick rise
unbleached white flour to make up total weight of 2 pounds 3 ounces
2 teaspoons salt mixed with 2 teaspoons water
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in the tepid water.
2. Place a paper lunch sack on a scale and add the barley and rye flour. Add enough additional unbleached flour to make up the total weight of 2 pounds 3 ounces.
3. Pour the yeast water into an electric mixer bowl and add 4 cups of the flour mixture. Use a KitchenAid or similar electric mixer for this process. Add the honey and olive oil, then mix to form a sponge or very soft dough. Mix by hand or with a machine until the dough begins to pull away from the side of the bowl, about 10 minutes. Add the salt mixed with water and continue mixing until the dough is smooth and elastic.
4. Knead the dough until it is smooth and elastic. Place on a plastic surface and cover with a large bowl. Allow to rise until double in bulk, then punch down and cover. Let rise again for a second proofing.
5. Punch down the dough and shape into 2 or 3 loaves. Place on a baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal. Dust the loaves with flour and allow to rise again until doubled in size.
6. Bake at 450°F (230°C) for about 25-35 minutes, or until the bread is a rich dark brown and sounds hollow when tapped with your finger. Allow to cool on cake racks before slicing.
